title: The Engineer
description: The Regulator is defeated — not by force, but by unpredictability. The Busker plays a melody the Mechanism couldn't calibrate for. The party learns they've been outside projected parameters since World 2. The Regulator won't confirm or deny what they are. But the eyes are the same.

lines: The Regulator stepped back from the center of the chamber. The platforms settled. The steam vents went quiet.
The Mechanism continued turning — it would always continue turning — but the fight was over.

lines.speaker: Regulator
lines.text: You did something I didn't model for. At the end. The music.

lines.speaker: Regulator
lines.text: You've been outside projected parameters since the second world. I adjusted. You exceeded the adjustment. I adjusted again. You exceeded that.

lines.speaker: Regulator
lines.text: You have been consistently outside the tolerance range. This world was supposed to bring you back within it.

lines.speaker: Regulator
lines.text: This world served its purpose. As did the others.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: You keep saying that. What does it mean?

lines.speaker: Regulator
lines.text: It means the work here is done. And the work elsewhere is beginning.

steps.text: They didn't say: I am the Calibrant. They didn't say: I designed all of this. They said what an engineer says when a project phase is complete: the work is done. The work elsewhere is beginning.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: The blueprints in the drafting room. The same handwriting on all five worlds.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: The next world. What do we find there?

lines.speaker: Regulator
lines.text: You'll find out when you arrive. That's how it works.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: You said 'always.' That's how it's always worked.

steps.text: They corrected the tense without acknowledging the correction. They turned back to the console. Made one more adjustment. And the world began to wind down.
